text: It is trendy to discuss Jevon's Paradox in AI (as AI gets more efficient, overall use increases) but the current situation is much simpler: thanks to agents, token demand is surging and compute is supply constrained, at least for powerful models. That will be reflected in pricing

Cheap models are still getting cheaper, but the highest end models increasingly offer a combination of intelligence, judgement, planning and correctness that make using them, rather than cheap models, extremely valuable. 

And because they are valuable, companies can charge more.
